card_description: Leibniz (German: "Unknown") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the philosophical tradition, Leibniz is 17th century rationalist; monads and pre-established harmony. PKD references Leibniz on possible worlds and the best of all possible worlds. Appears in 4 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick uses Whitehead's and Leibniz's philosophy to argue that humans are "memory spools" or "perceiving cells" within the vast organism of God."
